ActionJob.description:
A scoped, time-boxed, single-use unit of remediation work (ADR-011 §Guards).

``items`` are the prior-state-bound plan items the actor re-verifies and acts on; the
signature (computed over :meth:`canonical_bytes`) covers every field including them, so a
tampered item set fails verification before any filesystem access (T-3).
ActionJob.title: ActionJob

AgentConfigOverrideIn.description:
Operator-set per-host config override (ADR-033 #10) — ONLY the safe, overridable fields.

``extra="forbid"`` makes the API reject any non-overridable key (host_id, ingest_url, cert
paths, secret refs, write_enabled), so the override boundary is enforced here AND re-enforced by
the owning agent, which re-validates the full merged config against its own model and applies it
fail-safe. ``throttle`` is opaque here (the agent re-validates it against its ThrottleProfile).
AgentConfigOverrideIn.title: AgentConfigOverrideIn

AgentRunReport.description:
End-of-run report an agent POSTs to ``/api/v1/agents/runs`` (mTLS-authenticated).

The host is the verified cert fingerprint, never the body (AR-0012). The server re-derives the
aggregate outcome (ok/partial/failed) and totals from ``scopes`` — it never trusts an
agent-asserted aggregate — so a misreporting agent can only describe its own scopes, not forge
a fleet-wide health verdict.
AgentRunReport.title: AgentRunReport

AgentRunResult.description: Acknowledgement of a recorded agent run (the server-derived outcome).
AgentRunResult.title: AgentRunResult

AgentRunScopeFrame.description: One scanned root's outcome within an agent run report (mirrors runner.ScopeOutcome).
AgentRunScopeFrame.title: AgentRunScopeFrame

AuditPage.description:
A keyset page of audit rows (newest first) plus the cursor for the next page.

``next_cursor`` is the ``id`` of the last row in this page (as a string), or null when the
final page has been reached — passed back as ``?cursor=`` to fetch older rows.
AuditPage.title: AuditPage

AuditRecordOut.description:
One hash-chained audit row for the Audit tab (GET /api/v1/audit).

``id`` is the append sequence; ``prev_hash``/``row_hash`` expose the tamper-evident chain so
the UI can show (and a verifier can re-walk) the linkage (ADD 03 §8).
AuditRecordOut.title: AuditRecordOut

AuditRecordPayload.description:
The actor's per-item mutation audit record, carried back over the result channel.

Core re-chains each onto the durable hash-chained store so the destructive act itself lands
on the tamper-evident log (ADR-025 §1; closes the deferred audit-threading TODO). The fields
mirror :class:`~fathom.core.audit.AuditRecord` exactly; ``prev_hash``/``row_hash`` reflect the
actor's own in-memory chain and are recomputed against core's live head on splice.
AuditRecordPayload.title: AuditRecordPayload

BrowseEntry.description: One directory entry in a browse listing — metadata only, never file contents.
BrowseEntry.title: BrowseEntry

BrowsePathIn.description: Operator request body: the absolute directory to list on the target host.
BrowsePathIn.title: BrowsePathIn

BrowseRequest.description:
A scoped, time-boxed, single-use authorisation to list exactly ONE directory (read-only).

The core signs this and the agent serves only the directory ``path`` names, after verifying the
signature (over :meth:`canonical_bytes`) covers every field — so a widened path / extended
expiry / swapped host invalidates it. The listing returns metadata only; file *contents* never
cross the channel. ``with_sizes`` asks the agent to compute a BOUNDED subtree size + file-count
per child dir (capped by ``size_max_entries`` and ``size_budget_ms``, flagged when truncated).
BrowseRequest.title: BrowseRequest

BrowseResult.description: The agent's reply to a :class:`BrowseRequest`: the listing of one directory (or an error).
BrowseResult.title: BrowseResult

BrowseVolume.description: One mounted volume from a live ``df`` probe (the Deploy df-style dropdown source).
BrowseVolume.title: BrowseVolume

BuildPlanRequest.description: Build a plan from a confirmed dedup group + the operator's explicit keeper (ADR-011).
BuildPlanRequest.title: BuildPlanRequest

ChangeOut.description:
One churn row — a created / modified / removed path in a window (ADD 09 §4).

Read-only, scope-filtered: the churn read for a path/window returns these straight from the
``change_log`` (incremental subsystem). ``size_delta`` is signed (negative on shrink/removal).
ChangeOut.title: ChangeOut

ClaimedBrowse.description: The agent-poll response: a signed browse request the agent should serve (else 204 idle).
ClaimedBrowse.title: ClaimedBrowse

ClaimedGrant.description: What the agent's poll route returns: the signed grant + the byte cap to read up to.
ClaimedGrant.title: ClaimedGrant

ClaimedJob.description:
What the poll route returns to the agent: the opaque ``job_id`` + the signed job to act on.

The ``job_id`` is a fresh correlation token (not the nonce — the nonce stays inside the signed
envelope); the agent echoes it back on the result so core can resolve the right awaiting call.
ClaimedJob.title: ClaimedJob

CreateAssignmentRequest.description: Grant a ``(role, scope)`` assignment to a user (ADD 13 §§2-3).
CreateAssignmentRequest.title: CreateAssignmentRequest

CreateUserRequest.description: Create a local user (federated users are provisioned on first login instead).
CreateUserRequest.title: CreateUserRequest

DeployBrowseRequest.description: Live browse on a not-yet-enrolled target (ADR-034 Phase 2): preflight conn + a path.
DeployBrowseRequest.title: DeployBrowseRequest

DuplicateGroupDetailOut.description: A duplicate group plus its (scope-filtered) members for the detail view.
DuplicateGroupDetailOut.title: DuplicateGroupDetailOut

DuplicateGroupOut.description:
A confirmed duplicate group with its non-binding suggested keeper (read-only).

``reclaimable_bytes`` counts only **native** copies (``size * (native_copies - 1)``) — a
network-mount member is a cross-mount alias and frees nothing, so it is excluded.
``suggested_keeper_*`` is the non-binding suggestion the UI shows with its reason (ADR-011).
Report only — no selection or write is implied (security_constraints: report-only boundary).
DuplicateGroupOut.title: DuplicateGroupOut

DuplicateMemberOut.description:
One member of a duplicate group — a single byte-identical copy (read-only).

``is_mount_alias`` flags a member that lives on a NETWORK mount (NFS/SMB/sshfs): it is a remote
*view* of bytes physically stored on another host, not a separate reclaimable copy, so the UI
highlights it as a cross-mount false positive and it does not count toward reclaimable space.
DuplicateMemberOut.title: DuplicateMemberOut

DuplicatesPage.description: A keyset-paginated page of duplicate groups (mandatory at 50M rows; API §2).
DuplicatesPage.title: DuplicatesPage

DuplicatesSummaryOut.description: Estate (or single-volume) dedup headline: how many groups and how much is reclaimable.
DuplicatesSummaryOut.title: DuplicatesSummaryOut

EnrollResponse.description: A pending TOTP enrollment (provisioning URI shown once; secret kept server-side).
EnrollResponse.title: EnrollResponse

EntryFrame.description:
One filesystem entry in a push batch (mirrors backends.FsEntry on the wire).

``partial_hash`` / ``full_hash`` are carried only on a ``mode='fullbit'`` batch; a metadata
batch leaves them ``None`` and ingest then leaves the catalogue columns untouched
(fullbit-dedup data_model_changes). Both are validated as 64-char BLAKE3 hex.
EntryFrame.title: EntryFrame

ExecResultPayload.description: One per-item execution outcome the actor returns (mirrors ``ExecResult``).
ExecResultPayload.title: ExecResultPayload

FileGrant.description:
A scoped, time-boxed, single-use authorisation to pull exactly ONE file (owner ruling).

The worker presents this (signed) to the agent over the agent-initiated channel; the agent
serves only the file matching ``(volume_id, inode)`` and re-stats to confirm the content
still matches ``content_hash`` (the worker never trusts a path the grant could be widened to).
The signature (over :meth:`canonical_bytes`) covers every field, so a widened path / swapped
inode / extended expiry invalidates it.
FileGrant.title: FileGrant

FinalizeResult.description:
Server acknowledgement of a post-drain rollup finalize (ADD 09 §8).

The agent posts this once after its drain so the server recomputes ``subtree_rollup`` (and a
``size_history`` point) for the calling host's volumes touched since the last finalize.
``volume_ids`` are exactly the volumes recomputed (empty when nothing changed since last
time); ``rollup_rows`` is the total rollup rows written across them. ``dup_groups`` is the
number of report-only duplicate groups rebuilt in the same call after a full-bit ingest (``0``
on a metadata-only deployment, where no full hashes exist — fullbit-dedup, ADD 02 §7.1). The
host is the authenticated mTLS identity, never the body (AR-0012).
FinalizeResult.title: FinalizeResult

FullBitScanRequest.description:
An operator's request to create a full-bit scan with a persisted impact ack.

Report-only: this records the operator's intent + acknowledgement (persisted on the snapshot
for audit, ADD 02 non-impact contract). It performs no content read and triggers no write —
the agent runs the gated full-bit pass on its own host (security_constraints).
FullBitScanRequest.title: FullBitScanRequest

GrowthPointOut.description: One downsampled point on the growth-over-time series.
GrowthPointOut.title: GrowthPointOut

GrowthSeriesOut.description: A server-downsampled growth series for one subtree (frontend ADD §10).
GrowthSeriesOut.title: GrowthSeriesOut

HistoryPointOut.description: One time-series sample of a subtree's size.
HistoryPointOut.title: HistoryPointOut

HostFrame.description: Agent self-description; the authoritative identity is the mTLS fingerprint.
HostFrame.title: HostFrame

HostOut.description:
One registered host + its agent liveness for the Agents/fleet tab (GET /api/v1/agents).

``volume_count`` is the number of catalogued volumes on the host; ``last_seen`` is the most
recent mTLS contact (null until the agent first pushes). Read-only fleet topology (ADD 04).
HostOut.title: HostOut

IngestBatch.description:
A single resumable, idempotent push from an agent (ADD 02 §7.2).

``removed_inodes`` carries the **explicit** deletions the incremental change feed detected
(incremental owner ruling: an explicit present/removed_at marker, NOT snapshot-staleness
inference). The server marks those ``(host_id, volume_id, inode)`` rows ``present=False`` and
emits a ``DELETE`` change_log row — it never deletes the catalogue row. Re-appearing inodes
resurrect on the next upsert. A metadata batch may carry removals; a fullbit batch never does
(full-bit re-hashes existing files, it does not detect deletions). The list is bounded by the
same per-batch limit as ``entries`` so a malformed batch cannot blow up the reconcile step.
IngestBatch.title: IngestBatch

IngestResult.description:
Server acknowledgement of an accepted batch.

``entries_removed`` is how many of ``removed_inodes`` matched a live catalogue row and were
flipped to ``present=False`` (incremental reconciliation). ``changes_logged`` is the number
of ``change_log`` rows the reconciliation emitted (0 when the volume's feed is disabled).
IngestResult.title: IngestResult

JobResultPayload.description:
The result an agent posts back for one dispatched job (drift for DRY_RUN, acts for EXECUTE).

``extra='forbid'`` so a malformed/over-broad result is rejected at the boundary. ``audit`` is
the actor's per-item act audit (empty for a dry-run); ``results`` the per-item exec status.
JobResultPayload.title: JobResultPayload

MeResponse.description: The authenticated principal + its effective grants/scopes.
MeResponse.title: MeResponse

MfaVerifyRequest.description: A TOTP code for step-up / enrollment confirmation.
MfaVerifyRequest.title: MfaVerifyRequest

OrganizeActivityOut.description:
Recent churn in a folder (ADR-021 Phase 3 watch trigger) — a hint to re-organise.

Read-only summary off the incremental change feed (``change_log``): how many files were
created/modified/deleted under ``path`` within the window. ``suggests_reorganise`` is True when
new or changed files appeared — the UI surfaces it as a "re-organise?" nudge; nothing is ever
auto-applied (a human still approves every plan).
OrganizeActivityOut.title: OrganizeActivityOut

OrganizeItemOut.description:
One file's proposed disposition after server-side validation/clamping.

``status`` is ``move`` (relocate/rename to ``proposed_relpath``), ``keep`` (already placed
well), or ``rejected`` (the model's target escaped the root, was a bad name, or collided —
never moved). ``proposed_relpath`` is relative to the requested folder.
OrganizeItemOut.title: OrganizeItemOut

OrganizeMoveIn.description: One operator-approved relocation: a catalogue entry + its in-root target (re-clamped).
OrganizeMoveIn.title: OrganizeMoveIn

OrganizePlanOut.description: A built, persisted MOVE plan — feed ``plan_id`` to the remediation dry-run/execute spine.
OrganizePlanOut.title: OrganizePlanOut

OrganizePlanRequest.description:
Build a reversible MOVE plan from a reviewed proposal's approved subset (ADR-023).

The client sends only ``entry_id`` + the approved ``dest_rel`` per move; the server pulls the
drift anchor (inode/size/hash) and source path from the catalogue and re-clamps every target to
``path`` (AR-0012 — the operator chose the destinations, the server owns the rest).
OrganizePlanRequest.title: OrganizePlanRequest

OrganizeProposalOut.description: A reviewable Organize suggestion for one folder (the model proposes; the server clamps).
OrganizeProposalOut.title: OrganizeProposalOut

OrganizeSuggestRequest.description: Ask for a proposed reorganisation of the files under one folder (read-only).
OrganizeSuggestRequest.title: OrganizeSuggestRequest

PlanAction.description: What to do with each file in a remediation plan.
PlanAction.title: PlanAction

PlanItem.description: One file to act on, with the prior state to re-verify against.
PlanItem.title: PlanItem

PreviewArtifactOut.description:
One DERIVED preview artifact for the browser (ADR-014). Never raw bytes/SVG/HTML.

``data_b64`` is the base64 of the *derived* artifact (a re-encoded raster, an extracted text
snippet, or a structured highlight) — already transformed by the sandbox. ``media_type`` is
the artifact's safe type, not the source file's. The wire model carries no raw original bytes
(security_constraints: preview is derived-only).
PreviewArtifactOut.title: PreviewArtifactOut

PreviewResultOut.description:
A rendered preview's derived artifacts + type badge + cache-hit metadata (read-only).

``type`` is the detected (magic-byte) type badge the UI shows; ``cache_hit`` distinguishes a
cached artifact from a fresh render; ``sandbox_job_id`` correlates with the access audit row
(file-mgmt §4.2). No raw bytes are present (ADR-014).
PreviewResultOut.title: PreviewResultOut

ProviderDuplicateGroupOut.description:
A set of objects the *provider* reports as identical, by hash algorithm (ADR-028 phase 2).

Distinct from :class:`DuplicateGroupOut` (content-verified BLAKE3): these come from a cloud
provider's own hash (rclone ``lsjson --hash``) at zero egress, and are **report-only** — there
is no suggested keeper and no remediation path (provider hashes never drive a delete).
ProviderDuplicateGroupOut.title: ProviderDuplicateGroupOut

ProviderDuplicateMemberOut.description: One member of a provider-hash duplicate group (read-only).
ProviderDuplicateMemberOut.title: ProviderDuplicateMemberOut

ProviderDuplicatesOut.description:
Provider-hash (cross-cloud) duplicate groups, capped at the requested limit.

``truncated`` is True when more groups exist than ``limit`` returned — the UI shows a "narrow
the scope / raise the limit" hint rather than implying it listed everything.
ProviderDuplicatesOut.title: ProviderDuplicatesOut

ReconcileOut.description: A read-only cross-host comparison: per-class counts + a bounded sample of flagged items.
ReconcileOut.title: ReconcileOut

ReconcileRequest.description: Compare a definitive ``(volume, path)`` against a comparison one (ADR-024; read-only).
ReconcileRequest.title: ReconcileRequest

RemoteTargetIn.description:
A remote scan target (rclone/SMB/SFTP) to generate into the agent bundle (ADR-029).

A thin wire model; the safety validation lives in :class:`RemoteTargetSpec` (mapped to a 422),
and the agent re-validates the rendered config. Credentials are references only (ADR-010).
RemoteTargetIn.title: RemoteTargetIn

RenderRequest.description:
One render job sent core→worker: the (already-fetched) raw bytes + the detected type + caps.

The bytes are base64'd; ``type`` is the magic-byte detection the core already did, but the
sandbox **re-detects** inside the container and never trusts this hint (defence in depth).
RenderRequest.title: RenderRequest

RenderResponse.description: The worker's reply: derived artifacts only (never the raw bytes back).
RenderResponse.title: RenderResponse

ResourceCaps.description:
Per-render CPU/memory/time/page + max-decompressed caps — the bomb guard (STRIDE D-6).

Config-driven (settings) so the concrete owner-set limits (1 CPU, 512 MiB, 10s, 50 pages,
100 MiB decompressed) are enforced at one place and the sandbox driver kills any render that
breaches them rather than letting a decompression/page bomb exhaust the host.
ResourceCaps.title: ResourceCaps

ScanCreatedOut.description: Acknowledgement that a full-bit scan request was recorded (no write performed).
ScanCreatedOut.title: ScanCreatedOut

ScopeMountIn.description: One scanned tree (agent path ← host path).
ScopeMountIn.title: ScopeMountIn

SearchResultOut.description: One estate-search hit (GET /api/v1/search): enough to render + jump to it in the explorer.
SearchResultOut.title: SearchResultOut

ServeRequest.description:
The agent's reply for one grant: the file bytes (base64), or an ``error`` it could not serve.

Exactly one of ``data_b64`` / ``error`` is set — a malformed reply that carries both or neither
is rejected at the boundary (``extra='forbid'`` + the validator) so it can never half-resolve a
pull.
ServeRequest.title: ServeRequest

ServerConfigOut.description:
The non-secret server feature flags shown (read-only) on the Settings page.

Curated by hand (never a blanket dump of Settings) so a secret can never leak: only feature
toggles, the inference model/URL, and numeric limits are listed here.
ServerConfigOut.title: ServerConfigOut

SignedBrowseRequest.description: A :class:`BrowseRequest` plus its detached Ed25519 signature and key id (base64; ADR-010).
SignedBrowseRequest.title: SignedBrowseRequest

SignedFileGrant.description: A :class:`FileGrant` plus its detached Ed25519 signature (base64) and key id (ADR-010).
SignedFileGrant.title: SignedFileGrant

SignedJob.description:
An :class:`ActionJob` plus its detached signature (base64) and key id (ADR-010).

The actor receives this over the agent-initiated outbound channel and must call
:func:`fathom.core.remediation.signing.verify_job` — never trust ``job`` directly.
SignedJob.title: SignedJob

SnapshotOut.description:
One immutable scan-run row for the Scans tab (GET /api/v1/scans).

Read-only history of metadata/full-bit scan runs on a volume. ``warning_ack`` carries the
operator's persisted impact acknowledgement for a full-bit run (ADD 02 non-impact contract).
SnapshotOut.title: SnapshotOut

SshCredentialIn.description: Transient SSH login material (never persisted; redacted in domain repr).
SshCredentialIn.title: SshCredentialIn

SupportedType.description: Renderable v1 preview types (ADR-014). Video/audio poster/cover-art deferred.
SupportedType.title: SupportedType

TopNItemOut.description: One 'biggest offender' row for the bar chart / top-N list.
TopNItemOut.title: TopNItemOut

TreeChildOut.description: A child node with aggregated subtree sizes + per-entry metadata for drill-down.
TreeChildOut.title: TreeChildOut

TreemapNodeOut.description: One sized node for the ECharts treemap/sunburst (one drill level, capped node set).
TreemapNodeOut.title: TreemapNodeOut

UserOut.description: A user record (never exposes the password hash).
UserOut.title: UserOut

VolumeFrame.description: The volume a batch belongs to (ADD 04 topology).
VolumeFrame.title: VolumeFrame

VolumeOut.description: A volume with usage and topology for the volumes view (bar/pie).
VolumeOut.title: VolumeOut

WireArtifact.description: A :class:`PreviewArtifact` with its derived bytes base64'd for JSON transport.
WireArtifact.title: WireArtifact

info.description: HTTP API for Fathomline (built on the Fathom engine). The read/query routes are RBAC- and scope-gated; the agent ingest routes authenticate by mTLS client-cert fingerprint behind the proxy; the remediation, preview and deployment surfaces are default-OFF and individually gated. See the project docs for auth and deployment.
info.summary: The storage-estate analyzer — read/query surface, agent ingest, and the default-OFF write/deploy surfaces.
info.title: Fathomline API

get.description: List registered hosts + agent liveness and volume count (scope-filtered, read-only).

schema.title: Response List Agents Api V1 Agents Get

post.description: Agent long-poll for the next signed browse request scoped to the calling host, or 204.
Deliver the directory listing (or error) for a browse request, scoped to the posting host.

schema.title: Response Serve Browse Result Api V1 Agents Browse Result Post

get.description:
Agent-facing (mTLS): THIS host's operator-set config override (ADR-033 #10), or 204 if none.

The agent GETs this at run start, merges it over its local config, RE-VALIDATES the whole result
against its own model, and applies it fail-safe (keeps the local config on any error). The host
is the verified cert fingerprint, never a body (AR-0012).

post.description:
Recompute subtree rollups (and rebuild dedup groups) after the calling host's drain.

Recomputes ``subtree_rollup`` for the host's freshly-ingested volumes (ADD 09 §8) and, when a
full-bit pass has landed content hashes, rebuilds the report-only duplicate groups in the same
transaction so the ``/duplicates`` view reflects the hashed content (the documented interim for
the arq ``dedup`` queue, ADD 02 §7.1). Carries the SAME mTLS + ingest-proxy-secret boundary as
``/ingest`` (``FingerprintDep``) and is NOT on the human-auth path: the host is the verified
cert fingerprint, never the body, and finalize only ever recomputes that host's volumes
(AR-0012). The agent calls this once after its drain; a host with nothing new since its last
finalize recomputes nothing and a metadata-only deployment rebuilds zero dup groups.
Accept a batch of fs_entry deltas from an authenticated agent.

Long-poll for the next signed job scoped to the calling host, or 204 if none.

Deliberately does NOT take the request-scoped DB session: it resolves the host in a transient
session, then long-polls holding no connection (pool-exhaustion guard for a fleet of pollers).
Correlate and resolve a job result; consume its nonce on the durable single-use ledger.

schema.title: Response Submit Job Result Api V1 Agents Jobs  Job Id  Result Post

post.description: Long-poll for the next signed file grant scoped to the calling host, or 204 if none.
Deliver the served bytes (or a failure) for a grant, scoped to the posting host.

schema.title: Response Serve Preview Grant Api V1 Agents Preview Grants Serve Post

post.description:
Record the calling agent's end-of-run outcome (fleet observability).

Same mTLS + ingest-proxy-secret boundary as ``/ingest`` (``FingerprintDep``): the host is the
verified cert fingerprint, never the body. The server re-derives the aggregate outcome from the
reported per-scope rows, so a misreporting agent can only describe its own scopes. Best-effort
from the agent's side — a 404-equivalent (unknown fingerprint) is reported as a no-op, never an
error, so run-reporting never destabilises an otherwise-good scan.

Operator: list a directory on ``host_id`` live (MANAGE_AGENTS + per-request step-up MFA).

Signs a short-TTL, single-use, host-scoped :class:`BrowseRequest`, enqueues it for the owning
agent, and returns the metadata listing the agent serves back. Read-only; audited. 503 when the
browse runtime is not provisioned; 504 when the agent does not answer within the request TTL.

put.description:
Operator (MANAGE_AGENTS): set this host's config override (ADR-033 #10), audited.

Shape-checked here (only the safe overridable fields; ``extra=forbid`` rejects identity/secret/
write_enabled) and RE-VALIDATED by the owning agent, which applies it fail-safe on its next run.
An empty/all-null body CLEARS the override. Scope-checked: a non-global principal can only
target hosts it can manage.

get.description:
Read a keyset page of the hash-chained audit log, newest first (READ_AUDIT; read-only).

Auditor|admin only. Paginated by descending ``seq`` (``cursor`` = the last ``seq`` of the
previous page) so the log stays browsable at scale; each row carries its ``prev_hash``/
``row_hash`` so the tamper-evident chain is visible to the UI.

post.description: Authenticate a local user and mint a session cookie (audited; no enumeration).
Revoke the current session (instant lockout) and clear the cookie (audited).

get.description: Return the authenticated principal and its effective grants/scopes.

post.description: Begin TOTP enrollment; the secret is stored by reference, never returned raw.
Verify a TOTP code and stamp step-up freshness on the session (ADD 13 §4).

get.description:
Complete the OIDC flow: validate the id_token, map groups→role, mint a session.

Guarded the same way as :func:`get_oidc_login`; id_token validation
(:func:`fathom.auth.providers.oidc.validate_id_token`) pins the signing-alg allow-list,
issuer and audience to defeat alg-confusion / audience-substitution.

Begin the OIDC authorization-code + PKCE flow against authentik (ADD 03 §2.1).

Returns 503 until the operator wires ``oidc_issuer`` / ``oidc_client_id`` and the client
secret (via Docker secret / OpenBao, never in code — ADR-010). The full redirect +
PKCE-challenge construction lands once the IdP wiring is configured; the SSRF-guarded
discovery primitives already live in :mod:`fathom.auth.providers.oidc`.

Return the churn feed ('what changed') for an in-scope subtree/window (scope-checked).

Gated by ``VIEW_METADATA`` like the rest of the read surface; the volume is scope-checked
before any churn row is read so an out-of-scope volume is rejected 403 (incremental subsystem).

schema.title: Response Get Changes Api V1 Changes Get

get.description: Return the non-secret server feature flags (read-only; env-controlled).

post.description:
List one directory on a not-yet-enrolled target over SSH (ADR-034 Phase 2; read-only).

DEPLOY_AGENT + a fresh per-request step-up MFA. Metadata only (never file contents); optional
bounded per-child subtree size. Lets the Deploy wizard pick scan roots/excludes before enrol.
Start a batch push-deploy (DEPLOY_AGENT + fresh MFA); returns the run to poll.
Issue a one-time pull-enrollment token + the bootstrap command (DEPLOY_AGENT + fresh MFA).

get.description:
Redeem a one-time enrollment token and return the agent bundle.

Linux grants get a gzip tarball; Windows grants get a zip (PowerShell ``Expand-Archive``;
Server 2016 has no ``tar.exe``). Token-only auth via the ``Authorization: Bearer`` header
(the target has no session): a single-use, short-TTL token validated server-side. Mints the
agent cert + renders the platform-appropriate bundle.

Stream the agent image archive for a *live* (non-consumed) enrollment token.

The image is not secret, so this does not spend the token (the bundle fetch does); but a live
token is still required so the multi-hundred-MB transfer is not an open endpoint. 404 if no
archive is configured. The bootstrap calls this only when the image is absent on the target.

post.description: Connect to one target and check Docker + proxy reachability (no change made).

List a target's mounted volumes via ``df`` (the Deploy df-style dropdown; read-only).

DEPLOY_AGENT + a fresh per-request step-up MFA — it opens an SSH session to the target.

schema.title: Response Probe Volumes Route Api V1 Deployment Probe Volumes Post

get.description: Return the live per-host status of a deploy run.
List duplicate groups (keyset-paginated, scope-filtered, read-only).

List cross-cloud provider-hash duplicate groups (zero-egress; report-only, ADR-028).

Declared before ``/duplicates/{group_id}`` so the literal ``provider`` segment is never parsed
as a group id. Scope-filtered (VIEW_DEDUP) — the RBAC predicate is pushed into the scan so no
out-of-scope member can appear. These groups come from the provider's own hash (no download)
and are **report-only**: there is no suggested keeper and no remediation path. Capped at
``limit`` (one extra is probed to set ``truncated``).

Return the in-scope dedup headline (group count + total reclaimable) for the dashboard.

Declared before ``/duplicates/{group_id}`` so the literal ``summary`` segment is never parsed
as a group id. Scope-filtered (VIEW_DEDUP); ``volume_id`` narrows to one volume.
Return one group with its in-scope members + non-binding suggested keeper (read-only).
Return time-series size samples for an in-scope subtree.

schema.title: Response Get History Api V1 History Get

get.description: Return a server-downsampled growth-over-time series for an in-scope subtree (ADD §10).

Summarise recent churn under ``path`` — a read-only "re-organise?" hint (ADR-021 Phase 3).

Reads the incremental change feed only; nothing is moved and nothing is auto-applied. Gated by
``organize_enabled`` + ``VIEW_METADATA`` + the server-authoritative scope, like /suggest.

post.description:
Build + persist a reversible MOVE plan from an approved subset (no FS touch; ADR-023).

Default-OFF behind BOTH gates; idempotent on ``idempotency_key``. The persisted ``plan_id`` is
then run through the remediation dry-run/execute spine (which carries the signed-job, drift, MFA
and blast-cap guards). This route only *builds* — it dispatches nothing.
Propose a reorganisation for the files under ``path`` (read-only; nothing is moved).
Render one file's bytes in an ephemeral gVisor sandbox and return derived artifacts.

get.description:
Render (or cache-hit) a single entry's DERIVED preview (PREVIEW cap + scope; audited).

Default-OFF + RBAC scope gate + audit-before-serve are all enforced before any artifact is
returned. The response carries derived artifacts only — never raw bytes (ADR-014).

post.description: Classify files under two roots by their shared relative path (read-only; ADR-024).
Build + persist a remediation plan (no FS touch). Idempotent on ``idempotency_key``.
Issue a signed DRY_RUN job and return the per-item drift preview (no mutation).

Execute the approved, non-drifted subset (EXECUTE_REMEDIATION + scope + FRESH MFA).

Default-OFF gate + server blast cap + dry-run-first are all enforced before any dispatch.
Purge a quarantined item after retention (QUARANTINE_MANAGE + step-up MFA; audited).

schema.title: Response Quarantine Purge Route Api V1 Remediation Quarantine  Item  Purge Post

post.description: Restore a quarantined item (QUARANTINE_MANAGE + step-up MFA; audited).

schema.title: Response Quarantine Restore Route Api V1 Remediation Quarantine  Item  Restore Post

get.description:
List scan-run history (newest first), scope-filtered; optionally narrowed to one volume.

Server-authoritative scope (ADD 13 §4): a non-global principal sees only snapshots on in-scope
hosts/volumes, with the system-volume gate (AR-011) applied via ``Volume.kind``. When
``volume_id`` is given it is scope-checked first so an out-of-scope volume is 403'd, not
silently empty.

schema.title: Response List Scans Api V1 Scans Get

post.description: Record a full-bit scan request + impact ack on a snapshot (report-only, no write).

get.description:
Find live entries by name across in-scope volumes (biggest first); jump-to in the explorer.

Gated by ``VIEW_METADATA`` and scope-filtered server-side (ADD 13 §4). When ``volume_id`` is
given it is scope-checked first (out-of-scope → 403), so a search is never silently empty for an
authorisation reason.

schema.title: Response Search Api V1 Search Get

get.description: Return the N largest immediate children of ``path`` (capped) — the 'biggest offenders'.

schema.title: Response Get Top N Api V1 Top N Get

get.description: Return the immediate children of ``path`` with aggregated subtree sizes (scope-checked).

schema.title: Response Get Tree Api V1 Tree Get

get.description: Return the largest children of ``path`` (capped) for the ECharts treemap/sunburst.

schema.title: Response Get Treemap Api V1 Treemap Get

get.description: List all users (admin).

schema.title: Response List Users Api V1 Users Get

post.description: Create a local user with an Argon2-hashed password (audited).
Grant a ``(role, scope)`` assignment to a user (audited; ADD 13 §8).

delete.description: Revoke an assignment (audited; instant effect on the next request).

get.description: List in-scope volumes with usage and storage topology.

schema.title: Response Get Volumes Api V1 Volumes Get
